frameworks.name: decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: subjective certainty in ambiguous cues modulates feedback interpretation and learning

frameworks.name: reversal learning under uncertainty
frameworks.one_line: confidence enhances updating when reward contingencies change

frameworks.name: chemogenetic circuit dissociation
frameworks.one_line: ACC vs BLA inhibition reveals separable roles in confidence and learning

core_question: Do anterior cingulate cortex and basolateral amygdala play dissociable roles in decision confidence and confidence-driven learning under uncertainty?
key_assumptions: post-choice waiting time is a valid proxy for decision confidence
ambiguous visual orientations parametrically modulate confidence
chemogenetic silencing causally isolates regional contributions
key_findings: waiting times scaled with discrimination accuracy, validating the confidence proxy
BLA silencing shortened waiting times overall without disrupting confidence sensitivity
ACC silencing made waiting times insensitive to confidence-modulating stimulus attributes
both ACC and BLA inhibition blocked confidence-driven enhancement of reversal learning
interpretations: ACC contributes to confidence computations whereas BLA contributes to overall waiting/persistence
ACC and BLA modulate distinct components of confidence-guided learning — strategy and rule consistency
